pi_items.source: CDC and AAP medical literature. In healthy newborns, pre-ductal SpO2 is 98.8±1.4% (preterm) or 98.9±1.3% (term). Mean normal SpO2 in newborn infants over 24 hours is 97%-98%. See: https://www.cdc.gov/heart-defects/hcp/screening/index.html and https://www.aap.org/en/patient-care/congenital-heart-defects/newborn-screening-for-critical-congenital-heart-defect-cchd/

AAP CCHD Screening Guidelines. SpO2 <90% is the fail threshold for CCHD screening (immediately abnormal). Historical CCHD studies show median post-ductal SaO2 of 90% in CCHD populations. The difference of 98-90=8% exceeds the >3% differential that indicates positive screening. See: https://publications.aap.org/pediatrics/article/155/1/e2024069667/200337/Newborn-Screening-for-Critical-Congenital-Heart and https://www.cdc.gov/heart-defects/hcp/screening/index.html
System configuration parameter, verified in setup_task.sh (line 35) and export_result.sh (line 14)
System configuration parameter, verified in setup_task.sh (line 34) and export_result.sh (line 15)

privileged_info_summary: The CCHD screening task uses clinically validated target values. The pre-ductal SpO2 target of 98% represents a normal healthy newborn baseline (expected range 97-98.9% in medical literature). The post-ductal SpO2 target of 90% represents the fail threshold in CCHD screening—any value <90% is automatically abnormal. The 8% differential (98% - 90%) exceeds the >3% threshold that indicates clinically significant ductal shunting suggestive of critical congenital heart disease. These values align with AAP and CDC CCHD screening guidelines. The task data is synthetic (simulated devices within OpenICE, an open-source medical device interoperability platform developed by Harvard's MD PnP program), and the deliverables are a configuration report and screenshot evidence.
